title: The Frustrating UI Maze
description: An adversarial puzzle game where the UI actively fights back — walls trigger collision shake, the finish button runs from your cursor using evasive vector math, controls invert at the midline, and proximity traps deploy blocking barriers.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Rigorous collision maze with mouseenter wall traps triggering shake animation, sarcastic fail messages, and rage quotient tracking
Evasive finish button using 2D Euclidean distance threshold (120px sensory radius) with vector-angle opposite translation and corner-escape teleportation
Dynamic coordinate axis inversion field at maze midline — custom cursor inverts delta motion below the boundary
Proximity barrier trap that deploys a blocking wall when cursor enters a defined zone
Custom cursor dot rendered via CSS with hidden system cursor (cursor: none) for full control
Glitch flash overlay, terminal shake keyframes, and scanning overlay for cyberpunk aesthetic
Attempt counter, rage quotient gauge, and passive-aggressive alert banner with rotating message pool
Full-screen victory overlay with stats and randomized mocking win messages
